ogTitle: Fernbank Creek vs Fernhill: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fernbank Creek and Fernhill.
heading: Fernbank Creek vs Fernhill — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fernbank Creek, New South Wales and Fernhill, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fernbank Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Fernhill (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fernbank Creek or Fernhill safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fernbank Creek has a flood risk score of 55/100, while Fernhill scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fernbank Creek scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Fernhill scores 17/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fernbank Creek or Fernhill?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fernbank Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
